source_section.title_label_raw: Development Agreements Development on a Registered Heritage Property

sections.title_label_raw: Agreements Development on a Registered Heritage Property

provisions.heading_context_raw: Agreements Development on a Registered Heritage Property
provisions.text: Development that is not otherwise permitted in this By-law may be permitted by development agreement on a registered heritage property that is not contained within a heritage conservation district, in accordance with Policy CHR-7 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Agreements Development on a Registered Heritage Property
provisions.text: Where development is permitted by development agreement on a registered heritage property, incentive or bonus zoning shall be required in accordance with the requirements of Part XV. Change to a Less Intensive Non-Residential Non-Conforming Use

sections.title_label_raw: On a lot that existed on the coming into force date of this By-law, the

provisions.heading_context_raw: On a lot that existed on the coming into force date of this By-law, the
provisions.text: On a lot that existed on the coming into force date of this By-law, the change of a non- residential non-conforming use in a structure or a non-residential non-conforming use of land to a less intensive non- residential non-conforming use of structure or land may be permitted by development agreement, in accordance with Policy IM-19 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22) Expansion of a Non-Residential Non-Conforming Use

sections.title_label_raw: In accordance with Policy IM-19 of the Regional Centre Secondary

provisions.heading_context_raw: In accordance with Policy IM-19 of the Regional Centre Secondary
provisions.text: In accordance with Policy IM-19 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy, where a non-residential non-conforming use in a structure is located on a lot that existed on the coming into force date of this By-law, Council may, by development agreement, allow the structure containing the non-residential non-conforming use to be extended, enlarged, or altered. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22) Relocation of a Non-Conforming Use to Support Public Investments

sections.title_label_raw: In accordance with Policy IM-20 of the Regional Centre Secondary

provisions.heading_context_raw: In accordance with Policy IM-20 of the Regional Centre Secondary
provisions.text: In accordance with Policy IM-20 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy, where a non-conforming use exists on a lot that is needed for the acquisition of land by the Province of Nova Scotia, the Halifax Dartmouth Bridge Commission, or the Municipality for public purposes, Council may, by development agreement allow the same use as the non-conforming use of land on an adjacent lot. Development in the King's Wharf (KW) Special Area

sections.title_label_raw: Development within the King's Wharf (KW) Special Area, as shown

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development within the King's Wharf (KW) Special Area, as shown
provisions.text: Development within the King's Wharf (KW) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3A, beyond what is permitted in the applicable zone, may be permitted by development agreement in accordance with Policies D-10 and IM-31 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy. Development in a CDD-2 Zone

sections.title_label_raw: Development that is not otherwise permitted in this By-law may

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development that is not otherwise permitted in this By-law may
provisions.text: Development that is not otherwise permitted in this By-law may be permitted within the CDD-2 zone by development agreement, in accordance with Policies F-4 and F-5 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development that is not otherwise permitted in this By-law may
provisions.text: Where development is permitted by development agreement within the CDD-2 zone, incentive or bonus zoning shall be required in accordance with the requirements of Part XV. Development in a CDD-1 Zone

sections.title_label_raw: Development that is not otherwise permitted within this By-law

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development that is not otherwise permitted within this By-law
provisions.text: Development that is not otherwise permitted within this By-law may be permitted in the CDD-1 zone by development agreement, in accordance with Policies F-4 and F-5 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development that is not otherwise permitted within this By-law
provisions.text: Where development is permitted by development agreement within the CDD-1 zone, incentive or bonus zoning shall be required in accordance with the requirements of Part XV. Development on an Existing Lot 1-Hectare or Greater

sections.title_label_raw: In a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, or CLI zone,

provisions.heading_context_raw: In a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, or CLI zone,
provisions.text: In a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, or CLI zone, development on lots with an area of 1.0 hectare or greater that existed on the coming into force date of this By-law may be considered by development agreement, in accordance with Policy UD-13 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy.

provisions.heading_context_raw: In a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, or CLI zone,
provisions.text: Where development is permitted by development agreement on lots 1.0 hectare or greater, incentive or bonus zoning shall be required in accordance with the requirements of Part XV. Development in the Robie Street (RS) Special Area

sections.title_label_raw: Development in the Robie Street (RS) Special Area, as shown

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development in the Robie Street (RS) Special Area, as shown
provisions.text: Development in the Robie Street (RS) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, beyond what is permitted by the applicable zone, may be considered by development agreement in accordance with Policies IM-37, IM-38, and IM-39 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development in the Robie Street (RS) Special Area, as shown
provisions.text: Where development is permitted by development agreement within the Robie Street (RS) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, incentive or bonus zoning shall be required in accordance with the requirements of Part XV. Schmidtville Heritage Conservation District

sections.title_label_raw: On the lot identified as the Schmidtville Historic Park and Institutional

provisions.heading_context_raw: On the lot identified as the Schmidtville Historic Park and Institutional
provisions.text: On the lot identified as the Schmidtville Historic Park and Institutional

provisions.heading_context_raw: On the lot identified as the Schmidtville Historic Park and Institutional
provisions.text: Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, any new main building or any addition to an existing main building larger than 139.5 square metres shall only be considered by development agreement, in accordance with Policies CHR-16 and CHR-17 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy. Neighbourhood-Scale Uses and Institutional Uses within the ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, or CH-1 Zone

sections.title_label_raw: A neighbourhood-scale use or an institutional use may be considered

provisions.heading_context_raw: A neighbourhood-scale use or an institutional use may be considered
provisions.text: A neighbourhood-scale use or an institutional use may be considered by development agreement within the ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, or CH-1 zone, in accordance with Policy E-5 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy. Development in the Saint Patrick's Alexandra Site (SPAS) Special Area

sections.title_label_raw: Development in the Saint Patrick's Alexandra Site (SPAS)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development in the Saint Patrick's Alexandra Site (SPAS)
provisions.text: Development in the Saint Patrick's Alexandra Site (SPAS) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, beyond what is permitted in the applicable zone, may be considered by development agreement in accordance with Policies IM-40 and IM-41 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development in the Saint Patrick's Alexandra Site (SPAS)
provisions.text: Where development is permitted by development agreement within the Saint Patrick's Alexandra Site (SPAS) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, incentive or bonus zoning shall be required in accordance with the requirements of Part XV. Residential Development within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area

sections.title_label_raw: Within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown
provisions.text: Within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, new or expanded residential uses shall only be permitted by development agreement, in accordance with Policy ED-6 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown
provisions.text: Where development is permitted by development agreement within the Halifax Grain Elevator (HGE) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, incentive or bonus zoning shall be required in accordance with the requirements of Part XV. Development in the Spring Garden Road and Robie Street (SGRRS) Special Area

sections.title_label_raw: Development within the Spring Garden Road and Robie Street

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development within the Spring Garden Road and Robie Street
provisions.text: Development within the Spring Garden Road and Robie Street

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development within the Spring Garden Road and Robie Street
provisions.text: Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, beyond what is permitted in the applicable zone, may be considered by development agreement in accordance with Policies IM- 42, IM- 43, IM-44, IM-45, and IM-46 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Development within the Spring Garden Road and Robie Street
provisions.text: Where development is permitted by development agreement within the Spring Garden Road and Robie Street (SGRRS) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, incentive or bonus zoning shall be required in accordance with the requirements of Part XV. Development under Former Planning Policies

sections.title_label_raw: In accordance with Policy IM-33 and IM-33.5 of the Regional Centre

provisions.heading_context_raw: In accordance with Policy IM-33 and IM-33.5 of the Regional Centre
provisions.text: In accordance with Policy IM-33 and IM-33.5 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy, Council may consider applications for substantive amendments to existing development agreements for the matters outlined in Policies IM-33 and IM-33.5. (RC- May23/24;E-Jun 13/24)

sections.title_label_raw: Notwithstanding any other provision of this By-law, within the lands

provisions.heading_context_raw: Notwithstanding any other provision of this By-law, within the lands
provisions.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this By-law, within the lands designated as a special planning area, early tree removal, blasting, and earthworks may be considered, subject to the provisions of a development agreement in accordance with policy G-llA of the Regional Municipal Planning Strategy. (STF - June 30/22; Eff - July 15/22) Development in the Lake Banook Paddler's Cove (LB-PC) Special Area

sections.title_label_raw: Notwithstanding Section 75, development in the Lake Banook

provisions.heading_context_raw: Notwithstanding Section 75, development in the Lake Banook
provisions.text: Notwithstanding Section 75, development in the Lake Banook Paddler's Cove (LB-PC) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, may be considered by development agreement in accordance with Policy IM-47 of the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy. (RC-Dec 11/25;E-Feb 2/26) Site-specific development agreements 498. 7 Development on PID 00005181, identified as 2764-2778 Robie Street, may be permitted by development agreement in accordance with Policy IM-35.6 in the Regional Centre Secondary Municipal Planning Strategy. (RC-Sep 9/25;E-Oct 3/25)
